LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LAMIA ETTEFAGH'S MOTION FOR PRETRIAL DISCOVERY REGARDING DEFENDANTS FINANCIAL CONDITION.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING BUT ADDS WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONDUCT PUNITIVE DAMAGES DISCOVERY PER CIVIL CODE 3295(C) IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY SHE WILL PREVAIL ON HER PUNITIVES CLAIM. (ID.) ACCIDENT REPORTS ARE INADMISSIBLE. (VEH. CODE 20013.) CIVIL AND CRIMINAL COMPLAINTS ARE ALLEGATIONS, NOT EVIDENCE. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT)ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-24-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Set For Trial On Jun-17-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Pretrial Discovery Regarding Defendants Financial Condition
Off Calendar Feb-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sara Peters, Esq. 415-981-7210 of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger for Plaintiff.
Melissa O'Connor, Esq. 415-421-1100 of DKM Law Group for Defendant Sekula.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. The Court looks at a declaration. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LAMIA ETTEFAGH'S Motion For Pretrial Discovery Regarding Defendants Financial Condition.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling but adds without prejudice. Plaintiff's motion to conduct punitive damages discovery per Civil Code 3295(c) is DENIED. Plaintiff fails to establish a substantial probability she will prevail on her punitives claim. (Id.) Accident reports are inadmissible. (Veh. Code 20013.) Civil and criminal complaints are allegations, not evidence. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For Pretrial Discovery Regarding Defendants Financial Condition
Off Calendar Jan-18-2019 Continued To Feb-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Sara M. Peters of the law offices of Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Schoenberger (415-981-7210) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Melissa L. O'Connor of the law offices of DKM Law Group (415-813-7661) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

No stipulation to Judge Pro Tem by moving party.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides.

PLAINTIFF LAMIA ETTEFAGH'S Motion For Pretrial Discovery Regarding Defendants Financial Condition is CONTINUED to February 8, 2019 at 9:30 AM in Deparmtent 302/Law and Motion. Further notice is waived. Judge Pro Tem Minnis shall issue recommendation to the court.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis: Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (Disc302)
